title: Main pages in repository
content: Primary pages include index.html, healthcare.html, education.html, culture.html, sporting-venues.html, harris-school-of-medicine.html, johnson-faculty-of-business.html, nbcc.html, state-university.html, and stadium-project.html.

title: Healthcare detail pages
content: Healthcare detail pages are brightwellmedicalcenter.html, campusofcare.html, cancerresearch.html, generalhospital.html, kensargenthouse.html, and kensargenthouse-standalone.html under the healthcare/ folder.

title: Panorama viewer interaction model
content: Healthcare detail pages typically use select#panoSelect and iframe#panoFrame. Selecting an option updates iframe src. Some pages also let clicking naming-table rows with data-pano update both iframe and active row state.

title: Cancer Research story experience
content: The Cancer Research campaign includes dynamic story content tied to campaign interactions. The page updates a story panel based on selected content, supporting immersive donor storytelling.

title: Ken Sargent House external experience bundle
content: Ken Sargent House pages use an external ISHARE embed bundle to support additional interactive campaign experience.

title: Primary intent of platform
content: The platform positions itself as immersive campaign communication for fundraising: helping donors explore projects spatially, understand impact, and engage through interactive storytelling.

title: Full project scope for assistant
content: The assistant should treat ISHARE as a full-project knowledge domain, covering all sectors, category pages, detail pages, campaign offerings, and visitor-facing content.

title: Sector recommendation policy
content: When users ask for campaign recommendations, first suggest sectors such as Healthcare, Education, Culture, and Sporting Venues based on user intent, then provide campaign links.

title: Cost sorting policy
content: For budget-driven prompts, sort campaigns or opportunities by known numeric costs from pricing data. If a campaign has no explicit price in source pages, mark cost as not specified instead of guessing.

title: Link-first answer policy
content: When returning campaign options, provide direct internal page links whenever available. Include external action links only when user intent asks for donation, AI, or immersive action endpoints.

title: Cross-sector campaign index source
content: knowledge-base/campaign-catalog.json is the normalized source for sectors, campaign links, and known min/max costs to support filtering and sorting in Pickaxe.

title: Healthcare sector content profile
content: Healthcare is the strongest sector for pricing-based campaign discovery in the current knowledge base. It includes multiple detail pages with known opportunity ranges and immersive presentation patterns.

title: Education sector content profile
content: Education includes multiple project pages such as Johnson Faculty of Business, Harris School of Medicine, State University, and NBCC. Pricing is not explicitly specified in the current source pages.

title: Culture sector content profile
content: Culture is represented by the Holodomor 1932-1933 Virtual Exhibit, positioned as an immersive heritage and public-history storytelling experience. Pricing is not specified in the current source page.

title: Sporting Venues sector content profile
content: Sporting Venues is represented by the Stadium project, focused on venue storytelling and supporter engagement. Pricing is not specified in the current source page.

title: Known Healthcare campaigns with pricing coverage
content: Known Healthcare campaigns with pricing coverage include General Hospital, Ken Sargent House, Campus of Care, and Cancer Research. Brightwell Medical Center is present, but explicit pricing values were not captured in the current knowledge base snapshot.
